overlines.classes: font-sans text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-[#D4B36A] font-semibold

typography.pairing_strategy: The Old Money Tech: Classic Serif Head (Cormorant Garamond) + Sterile/Clean Sans Body (DM Sans). This establishes a premium hospitality and concierge aesthetic.

colors.strategy: Warm white canvas (#F4F1EC) to match the landing page. High contrast near-black (#0B0B0D) for primary text and the hero case card. Gold (#D4B36A) used sparingly for active states, progress, and highlights. DO NOT make the main background dark.

visual_enhancers.shadows: Diffuse, soft ambient shadows on light cards. Example: `shadow-[0_8px_32px_rgba(11,11,13,0.06)]`.
visual_enhancers.borders: 1px delicate inner stroke. `border-[#D4B36A]/20` on the dark hero card to give it a premium etched look. `border-[#0B0B0D]/10` on light cards.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Use 'Crystal Glass' for the bottom nav. High opacity for richness (`bg-[#F4F1EC]/85`), heavy blur (`backdrop-blur-xl`), 1px subtle top border (`border-t border-[#0B0B0D]/5`).
visual_enhancers.texture: Optional subtle noise/grain overlay to kill digital flatness and enhance the luxury hotel feel.

layouts.page: Mobile-first priority. Generous spacing. Use `p-6` or `p-8` container padding. Never center everything.
layouts.cards: Subtle roundness (`rounded-2xl` or `rounded-[20px]`). Dense internal padding (`p-6`).
layouts.hero_card: The active booking case is the hero. Use a dark background (`bg-[#0B0B0D]`) for this specific card on top of the warm white page background to create a commanding, high-contrast presence.
layouts.pills: Action pills should be `rounded-full` with a delicate border (`border border-[#0B0B0D]/15`), transparent background, text `#0B0B0D`. Hover state fills with light gold/cream.

motion.hover: Interactive cards/buttons should have a slight lift (`-translate-y-1`), a smooth shadow transition, and border color shift.
motion.entrance: Staggered fade-in and slide-up for the dashboard elements on load.
motion.tab_nav: Micro-interactions on tab click. Icon scale bump.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure #D4B36A text has sufficient contrast against #0B0B0D (dark card) and #F4F1EC (light bg). Use text shadows over images.
accessibility.focus: Use `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#D4B36A] focus:outline-none` for keyboard navigation.
accessibility.data_testid: MANDATORY on all interactive elements, navigation tabs, action pills, and case cards (e.g., `data-testid="active-case-card"`).

images.description: Couple standing in front of a spanish-style building, used as an elegant background for the Explore Venues banner to evoke luxury.
Professional portrait of a man in a suit, used for the dedicated Relationship Manager (RM) avatar.

active_case_hero.design: Dark card (`bg-[#0B0B0D]`) with warm white text (`text-[#F4F1EC]`). Use `#D4B36A` (gold) for stage progress bars/indicators. Use `Cormorant Garamond` for the event/venue name to make it feel like an exclusive reservation. This contrasts starkly but beautifully against the warm white page background.

rm_info_card.design: A clean white (`bg-[#FFFFFF]`) or slightly translucent card with a delicate 1px border (`border-[#0B0B0D]/10`). Displays the RM avatar, name, role (Concierge), and subtle action icons (phone/message).

action_pills.design: A horizontal scrollable list of quick actions (Messages, Payments, Shared). Pill-shaped (`rounded-full`), delicate border, `DM Sans` text. Elegant and simple.

explore_cta.design: Banner spanning the content width. Uses the architectural venue image as background with a dark gradient overlay at the bottom for text legibility. Features a serif headline 'Explore Premium Venues' and a minimal gold arrow.

bottom_nav.design: Sticky navigation at the bottom. Background is `bg-[#F4F1EC]/90` with `backdrop-blur-xl`. The active tab icon turns `#D4B36A` and text turns `#0B0B0D`. Non-active tabs are `#0B0B0D/40`.

instructions_to_main_agent: Redesign `CustomerHome.js` to completely drop the full-screen black background.
Set the main page container background to `#F4F1EC` (warm white) so it visually reconnects with the landing page and venue pages.
Make the Active Case Card the absolute focal point by giving it a solid `#0B0B0D` (near black) background. This card is a premium dark accent piece sitting on a warm white canvas.
Ensure all major display headings (like 'Welcome back, [Name]', the event name on the card, and the Explore banner) use `Cormorant Garamond`.
Body text, metadata, labels, and buttons must use `DM Sans`.
Implement soft, diffused ambient shadows for light elements, and crisp inner strokes (`border-white/10`) for the dark active case card.
Ensure the bottom navigation perfectly blends with the light theme, using glassmorphism (`bg-[#F4F1EC]/90 backdrop-blur-xl`), but ABSOLUTELY NO pure transparent backgrounds.
Space elements out generously. Use `gap-6` or `gap-8` between major sections (Greeting, Case Card, RM Info, Action Pills, Explore).
Add `data-testid` to every single interactable element, button, tab, and key data display card.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
DO NOT use complete `transparent` background in header, overlays, any surface that has content under it.
